evidence.title: The First BookTok Trend Roundup of 2026 - by Alyssa Morris
evidence.content:
# The First BookTok Trend Roundup of 2026

### If you guessed it's mostly Heated Rivalry you wouldn't be wrong

Alyssa Morris

Jan 09, 2026

managed to squeeze in one last trip to my favorite place to read in 2025

Welcome back to Romancing the Phone, and welcome to 2026 and a new year on BookTok. We’ve entered the dawn of a beautiful new era, a time when someone made a wildly successful, beautifully earnest, emotionally deep, free from shame adaptation of a series of romance novels that is getting the reception it deserves (world domination and widespread psychosis). As I’ve already said, it feels too early to say what the exact impact of this one will be, but I can’t wait to find out. [...] She also raises the point of “cringe,” an increasingly present concern for some readers in conversations about romance. I, personally, work very hard to kill the part of me that cringes, but I do think some of the desire for yearning can be tied to this impulse as well. If characters are pining away from each other and observing, then they aren’t having as much earnest dialogue with each other. More to think about here!

Even Navessa Allen weighed in:

 is hard! Hats off to the creators.

# The People We Meet on Vacation Movie

source [...] #### Discussion about this post

Amandine B

Jan 10

Alyssa, any thoughts on the emerging trend for more upmarket love stories (with or without a HEA) as opposed to “straight up” romances for 2026? Karin Gillespie of Pitch Your Novel writes about it in her latest Substack post and I’d love your take on it, too. I wonder if this trend would address the need for more yearning and less high spice you mention here. Thanks!

ReplyShare

1 reply by Alyssa Morris

Jan 10

This is better and more fun than scrolling this myself!

ReplyShare

6 more comments...

No posts

### Ready for more?

evidence.query: United States webnovel platform publishing marketing 현대 로맨스 Wattpad Tapas
evidence.title: Top 10 Global Digital Platforms for Web Novels and Creative Writing Content – ALL THE FACTS ABOUT THE WRITING WORLD
evidence.content:
Key Benefits:

1. WattPad give access to a massive global audience with mobile-first reach.
2. More focused on community interaction. Here, writers receive feedback directly from readers and make changes.
3. It provides editorial support, marketing help, and brand partnership access.
4. It provides monetisation opportunities, like the Wattpad Creators Program, Traditional publishing deals and Film or TV adaptations.
5. It is a multilingual platform with diverse genre representation.

Ultimately, Wattpad is a powerful multi-format publishing platform. However, it requires strong engagement to achieve a steady income. That makes it more favourable for already-popular writers.

### 3. Tapas [...] Key Benefits:

1. Publishing in Tapas is free, and creators retain full ownership of their work.
2. It provides a creator-first dashboard to track performance, schedule posts, and build readership.
3. It is a global community where fans can interact with stories through comments and subscriptions.
4. Offers multiple genres and formats.
5. Shares monetisation opportunities through ad revenue, affiliate marketing, publishing, adaptations and direct fans support.

Tapas is a hybrid storytelling platform where the comics ecosystem is currently stronger and more established. It is a good choice for creators who want to publish webcomics to reach a global audience.

### 4. Scribble Hubs [...] ### 3. Tapas

Tapas is another renowned digital storytelling platform. It is best known for webcomics and web novels. According to the website, 80% of its content is webcomics.

Tapas is mainly designed for phone screens and uses an episodic structure. It has a global readership with 10 million registered users worldwide. Over 75,000 creators are publishing original stories and comics, with more than 100,000 series.

Tapas attracts a young audience. In fact, it is a good platform for female creators because its significant readers are women aged 18–24. Their most famous genres are romance, fantasy, supernatural, action, adventure, horror, comedy, and drama.

Key Benefits:

evidence.title: AI Web Novel Plot: How to Build Compelling Web Fiction with AI (May 2026)
evidence.content:
Genre trend research — identify what's popular, what's oversaturated, and what gaps exist in your web novel's genre
 World-building research — pull historical, cultural, and scientific details that add authenticity to your settings
 Reader sentiment analysis — surface Reddit threads and forum discussions about reader preferences and pet peeves in your genre

### 🎨 Graphic Designer

Visual design partner for creating character reference sheets, world maps, cover concepts, and scene mood boards that keep your story's visual identity consistent — especially valuable for web novels that publish with cover art on serialization platforms. [...] > The web novel market is experiencing a robust 14.2% CAGR, driven by mobile reading platforms in Asia, Latin America, and increasingly the West — but reader expectations are rising just as fast. Readers on platforms like Royal Road, Webnovel, and Kakao Page are ruthless about plot holes, power-scaling inconsistencies, and dropped storylines.

### The Serialization Treadmill Destroys Quality [...] > "@Real-Time Search What are the most common complaints readers have about cultivation web novels on Royal Road and Webnovel? I want to avoid the biggest tropes that turn readers off."

## Results & Use Cases

### 📊 Solo Author Publishing Daily on Royal Road

Scenario: A web novel author writing a LitRPG progression fantasy needs to maintain a daily chapter release schedule (1,500–2,500 words per day) while keeping power scaling, quest systems, and 30+ named characters consistent across 400+ chapters.

evidence.query: United States cultural sensitivity localization Korean web novel 현대 로맨스
evidence.title: Korean Web Novels in English: How to Translate Korean Web Novels Using AI in 2026 | Lexilit
evidence.content:
### Korean Web Novel Conventions

Korean web novels follow distinct genre conventions that differ significantly from Western fiction. Understanding these is crucial for accurate translation:

 Rofan (로판, Romance Fantasy): Female-led stories featuring reincarnation, transmigration, or regression into fantasy worlds, often with intricate political intrigue
 Munpia-style Fantasy: Epic male-led progression fantasies with detailed power systems and cultivation elements
 Hwangjin (황진, Imperial Romance): Historical romances set in Korean imperial courts with complex etiquette and power dynamics
 Heona (허나, Modern Fantasy): Contemporary settings with supernatural elements, common in thriller and horror genres

## Popular Types of Korean Novels People Want in English [...] Custom AI Agents for Korean Fiction: Configure translation agents specifically for Korean novel genres — whether you're translating a formal historical romance set in the Joseon dynasty or a fast-paced modern progression fantasy
 Honorific Preservation: Lexilit's AI understands Korean speech levels and can convey the social dynamics encoded in honorifics through English word choice and sentence structure
 One-Click Import: Paste a link to a Korean web novel or paste the raw Korean text, and Lexilit fetches your content instantly — ready to translate
 Publication-Ready Export: Export your translated Korean novel as PDF, EPUB, or TXT — ready for reading on Kindle, Apple Books, or any device [...] ### Korean Web Novel Platforms

 Munpia (문피아): One of Korea's largest web novel platforms, featuring thousands of serialized novels across all genres. Free chapters available with premium content for subscribers
 Joara (조아라): A major Korean web novel platform known for romance, fantasy, and BL (Boys' Love) fiction. Large free-to-read library
 Novelpia (노벨피아): Growing platform popular for progression fantasy and regression/return genre novels
 KakaoPage (카카오페이지): Kakao's web fiction and webtoon platform with a massive Korean novel library. Many popular Korean novels are serialized here first
 Ridibooks (리디북스): Korea's leading e-book platform with extensive Korean novel collection, including both web novels and traditionally published fiction

### Korean Novel Aggregation and Discovery

evidence.title: World Wide Web novels: Stories Found Only Behind a Screen
evidence.content:
Webnovel genres

One of the differences between traditional print novels and web novels is the way their way of labelling genres.

For example, instead of categorising with single words like ‘romance’ or ‘fantasy’, the community of web novel writers/readers tend to rely on real time sharing of preferences and clichés.

So main genres including ‘alternative history’, ‘game fantasy’, ‘martial arts' and ‘modern talents’, are just a handful in the long list. There are also detailed sub-genres, which are just more specific to what you might be looking for in a novel, such as 'zombie', 'evil woman possession', apocalypse water' and 'card battle'/

Therefore, you can always find something that will be your cup of tea.

Authors and readers galore [...] Authors and readers galore

If you are aiming to write fiction, web novels provide more flexibility of content and greater publishing opportunities. Sites such as Webnovel, Wattpad, Royal Roadand Scribblehubhost works, and if your story becomes the talk of the active web novel community, it can grow into a very profitable hobby, since many of these sites have dedicated readers willing to pay for every chapter or for a subscription to get newly released chapters earlier than anyone else. [...] Sir Walter Scott's first major novel, Waverley (1814)

Web novels (stories published originally on websites) are in fact quite new in the timeline, with the earliest known form coming from China around the early 2000s. A culmination of Japanese and Korean online literature culture also contributed greatly. As an Anonymous user stated in a WebNovel Q&A: “Online novels first appeared in 1997... In the year 2000, online novels became popular in China and gradually developed into a huge industry.”

This became an upward trend as technology developed, and translations of these novels were spread across Asia and eventually into the West, for example, Sylver Seekerand Beware of Chicken (written by a Canadian).

Webnovel genres

작성 방향: 미국 현대 로맨스 독자를 겨냥할 때는 공개 웹 근거상 반복 노출되는 장르 키워드와 트로프를 초반 소개·태그·챕터 훅에 명확히 반영하는 편이 안전합니다.
검색 근거가 플랫폼 내부 전체 데이터가 아니므로, 수치 단정 대신 ‘공개 웹에서 관찰되는 경향’으로 표현해야 합니다.
감정선과 관계 진전 속도를 명확히 설계합니다.
동의(consent), 권력 불균형, 독성 관계의 미화 여부를 점검합니다.
트로프 태그를 제목·소개·마케팅 문구에 일관되게 반영합니다.
시장/독자 신호: BookTok/TikTok 관련 근거가 관찰되어 짧은 훅·트로프 중심 마케팅 문구가 중요합니다.
Wattpad 관련 결과가 포함되어 태그·장르 분류·연재형 독자 유입을 고려할 수 있습니다.
로맨스/로맨타지 트로프가 반복 노출되므로 관계 구도와 감정선 키워드를 전면에 배치합니다.
판타지·성장형 키워드가 보이면 세계관 규칙, 성장 보상, 회차별 목표를 빠르게 제시합니다.
문화 주의사항: 한국식 호칭, 위계, 가족/직장 문화는 직역보다 장면 맥락 안에서 기능을 설명하거나 현지 독자에게 익숙한 관계 표현으로 보완합니다.
현지 독자가 민감하게 받아들일 수 있는 성별 고정관념, 외모 기준, 권력 불균형 로맨스는 미화되지 않게 조정합니다.
플랫폼/마케팅: 검색 근거에 노출된 플랫폼/채널: Wattpad, Tapas, Radish, Kindle/Vella, Kakao 계열.
각 플랫폼의 최신 업로드 규정, 성인물 정책, 태그 체계를 업로드 직전에 별도 확인합니다.
플랫폼명은 추천 채널 후보이지, 해당 플랫폼 내부 데이터 분석 결과로 표현하지 않습니다.

warnings: 공개 웹 검색 결과는 전체 플랫폼 통계나 내부 순위, 모든 독자층을 대표하지 않습니다.
시장 수치나 성장률은 추정치이며, 정확한 플랫폼별 매출이나 이용자 수와 다를 수 있습니다.
장르 트렌드와 독자 반응은 빠르게 변할 수 있으므로 주기적 재검토가 필요합니다.
문화적 민감도는 현지 독자 반응에 따라 다를 수 있어 보수적 접근이 요구됩니다.
recommendations: 초기 기획 단계에서 미국 현대 로맨스 독자층의 인기 트로프와 감정선 패턴을 조사해 반영합니다.
마케팅 문구와 태그에 BookTok/TikTok에서 인기 있는 키워드를 포함시켜 노출을 극대화합니다.
Wattpad 등 연재형 플랫폼 특성을 고려해 회차별 목표와 독자 피드백 반영 체계를 마련합니다.
문화적 차이를 고려해 한국식 표현은 현지화하며, 권력 불균형이나 독성 관계 묘사는 신중히 다룹니다.
플랫폼별 최신 정책과 태그 체계를 업로드 직전에 반드시 확인하고 준수합니다.
